title: Base64 Encoder
shortDescription: Turn text into Base64 for headers, API payloads, test data, and developer notes.
metaDescription: Encode plain text to Base64 in your browser for headers, API payloads, test fixtures, and technical notes.
intro: Paste text and get a Base64 string you can copy into a header, request body, fixture, or script.
overview:
Base64 turns bytes into ASCII characters so text can pass through systems that expect character-safe data. It is often used when preparing Authorization values, encoding small request fragments, storing sample content in fixtures, or matching what another service returns.

This tool is for text input, so it is best suited to small and medium strings rather than files or binary blobs. The output is reversible: Base64 does not protect secrets, and anyone who receives the encoded string can decode it back to the original text.
howItWorks: Enter the exact text you want to encode.
The browser converts the text’s UTF-8 bytes into a Base64 string.
Copy the result into your API client, header, documentation, or test case.

useCases.title: Authorization header prep
useCases.description: Encode a user:password or client:secret pair before placing it in a Basic Authorization header for a request test.

useCases.title: Webhook payload samples
useCases.description: Represent small text fragments in Base64 when a receiving system expects encoded values inside a payload.

useCases.title: Fixture values for tests
useCases.description: Create stable encoded strings for snapshots, mocks, sample responses, and integration test fixtures.

useCases.title: Release notes and support cases
useCases.description: Share an encoded example in internal documentation when you need a reproducible text value without manual formatting.

examples.title: Readable phrase
examples.description: A straightforward text sample that makes the encoding pattern easy to verify.

examples.description: Shows the Base64 form of a colon-separated value commonly prepared before Basic Auth testing.

examples.title: Mini JSON payload
examples.description: Useful when a compact JSON fragment must be embedded as encoded text in a test or note.

examples.title: Unicode text
examples.description: Demonstrates that accented characters are encoded from their UTF-8 bytes, not stripped or normalized.

faq.question: Does Base64 hide the content of my text?
faq.answer: No. Base64 is only an encoding format. It can be reversed immediately, so it should never be treated as secret storage.

faq.question: Can I encode characters like accents, emojis, or non-Latin text?
faq.answer: Yes. The tool encodes the text as UTF-8 bytes, which preserves Unicode input such as ñ, ü, 漢字, and emoji.

faq.question: Why does my output end with one or two equals signs?
faq.answer: Padding is part of standard Base64 for many inputs. It helps align the encoded output to the expected block size.

faq.question: Can I paste the result directly into an HTTP header?
faq.answer: Yes, if the server expects Base64. For Basic Auth, the encoded value is usually written after the Basic prefix.

faq.question: What mistakes cause bad output?
faq.answer: Changing the input text, adding extra spaces, or encoding already-encoded text are the most common issues. If the receiving system expects raw bytes instead of text, the result will also be wrong.

faq.question: Is this tool suitable for large files?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for text conversion. Large files and binary data are better handled with file-oriented tools built for that purpose.

seo.title: Base64 Encoder
seo.description: Encode text to Base64 for headers, API payloads, fixtures, and technical examples.
